response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r2c5 is in critical survival mode with SoC at 20.9% (7.73/37 kWh) and ~5.4 hours of autonomy remaining under 1.0 kW load. The cluster is locked in a cascading deficit deadlock: r1c5, r2c4, and r3c5 are all critically depleted and have rejected all outbound supply requests over the past 90 minutes, citing self-preservation and systemic collapse risk. Repeated attempts at balanced (0.15 kW/tick) and conservative (0.05 kW/tick) sharing policies have failed to stabilize neighbor demand or trigger grid reconnection. The pattern of rejections from all peers indicates that geographic reciprocity is currently impossible—every household is below sustainable thresholds. The only viable path forward is aggressive load-shedding (target ≤0.5 kW) to extend autonomy, coupled with maximum-urgency inbound broadcast to attract supply from any available source (grid reconnection, external microgrids, or peers with surplus). Outbound sharing must be suspended except as a last-resort incentive if inbound supply materializes within the next 2 ticks.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.60
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.00
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Cluster-wide deficit deadlock; all peers critically depleted and rejecting supply. SoC 20.9% with 5.4h autonomy. Suspend outbound sharing; escalate load-shedding to ≤0.5 kW and broadcast maximum-urgency inbound requests. Grid reconnection or external supply required within 2 ticks to avoid cascade failure."
ttl_ticks: 2
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
